9.reason: [Incorrect use of isdigit: subtracting '0' from char before passing to isdigit is wrong. isdigit expects the raw character, not its numeric value. Should be isdigit(a) and isdigit(b).]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  The condition is incorrect; isdigit expects a character, not an integer. It should be isdigit(a) && isdigit(b).
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  The argument to isdigit should be the character itself, not a-'0'. Use isdigit(a) && isdigit(b) instead.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  error: should be isdigit(a) and isdigit(b)
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Error: should be `isdigit(a) && isdigit(b)`; a-'0' converts character to integer, not a character.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Incorrect use of isdigit: passing a-'0' shifts the character value, causing the check to fail for digits. Should pass the character directly.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [isdigit expects a character argument; subtracting '0' converts it to a numeric value that will fail the digit check. Should be isdigit(a) && isdigit(b)]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 1.0 , reason:  [Incorrect use of isdigit: subtracting '0' from char before passing to isdigit is wrong. isdigit expects the raw character, not its numeric value. Should be isdigit(a) and isdigit(b).]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Incorrect use of isdigit: should pass the character directly, not subtract '0'. isdigit(a) and isdigit(b) are correct. Subtracting '0' converts digit char to int, but isdigit expects char. Also, for non-digit chars, this causes undefined behavior.]

26.reason: When i=0, a[i-1] accesses index -1, which is out of bounds. Also, when i is the last index, a[i+1] accesses out of bounds.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  When i=0, a[i-1] accesses index -1, which is out of bounds. Also, when i is the last index, a[i+1] accesses out of bounds.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  When i=0, a[i-1] accesses index -1, which is out of bounds. Also, when i is the last index, a[i+1] accesses out of bounds. Need to check boundaries.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  error: missing bounds check; accessing a[i-1] or a[i+1] may be out of range when i=0 or i=n-1
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Error: when i==0, a[i-1] is out of bounds; when i==n-1, a[i+1] is out of bounds. Should check i>0 && i<n-1.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Missing boundary check: accessing a[i-1] and a[i+1] without verifying i>0 and i<n-1 causes out-of-bounds array access.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Accessing a[i-1] and a[i+1] without checking bounds (i>0 && i<n-1) causes out-of-bounds array access when i is 0 or n-1]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Potential out-of-bounds access: when i=0, a[i-1] is a[-1]; when i=n-1, a[i+1] is a[n] (null terminator or beyond). Should check bounds before accessing a[i-1] and a[i+1].]

37.reason: The loop does not output the first character (a[i-1]) and the last character (a[i+1]) of the expansion. It only outputs the middle characters, missing the start and end.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  The loop does not output the first character (a[i-1]) and the last character (a[i+1]) of the expansion. It only outputs the middle characters, missing the start and end.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  The loop overwrites final array starting at index i, but the characters before i are not stored in final. Also, the loop does not output the first character (a[i-1]) and the last character (a[i+1]) of the expansion.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  error: loop includes the last character, causing duplicate print; also j may exceed array bounds
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Error: loop includes the last character, causing duplication when later printing a[i+1] in the else branch. Should stop at `i+(a[i+1]-a[i-1])-1`.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Off-by-one error: the loop should print characters strictly between the start and end characters, so the iteration count should be reduced by 1 to avoid printing the end character twice.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [The expansion loop logic is flawed: it starts indexing at i for the final array, misses printing the start character a[i-1], and fails to skip expanded characters in the outer loop, causing duplicate and incorrect output]
